SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Aug-03-2009 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-15-13 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Settlement Conference. Comm: William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Citibank (South Dakota) N.A.
Defendant Thomas Katz was present in pro per and placed under oath.
Trial De Nova filed by defendant on March 29, 2009.
Case removed from Trial De Nova Calendar on April 2, 2009, and Trial set for August 3, 2009.

Settlement agreement of the parties was placed on the record by Mr. MacDonald. In accordance with the terms of the agreement, defendant Thomas Katz shall pay plaintiff Citibank (South Dakota) N.A. the sum of $18,284.00. Four thousand ($4,000.00) dollars of said amount shall be due on August 24, 2009, and payable to plaintiff's counsel, the law firm of Hunt & Henriques. The remaining balance shall be paid in equal installments, plus interest, over the next forty-eight (48) months, with each payment due no later than the 24th day of each month. If the 24th of any month falls on a weekend or holiday, defendant's payment shall be due the next business day.

Counsel for plaintiff shall submit a proposed judgment to the Court for signature and filing no later than August 12, 2009, but execution of said judgment shall be stayed provided defendant Thomas Katz continues to make his monthly payments in a timely fashion until the balance is paid in full.

Defendant Thomas Katz was voir dired by the Court as to the terms and his understanding of the agreement. Law firm of Hunt & Henriques shall prepare the proposed judgment.

Matter is continued to October 15, 2013 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 212 on the CRC 3.1385 Dismissal Calendar.

MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Citibank (South Dakota) N.A.'s Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted is granted. Notice And Motion To Compel Response To Interrogatories And Demand For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft
Off Calendar Jan-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Citibank (South Dakota) N.A.'s Motion To Compel Response To Interrogatories And Demand For Production Of Documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $312.50, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
